identitySentence: Navarrette Bowen PC is a boutique Denton family law firm where attorney Chrysandra Bowen, Board Certified in Texas Family Law and practicing since 2008, helps clients with divorce, child custody, and adoption throughout Denton County.

concept.headline: Divorce and Custody Guidance from a Board Certified Denton Family Law Attorney
concept.subheadline: Whether you are worried about the house, your children, or assets you spent a lifetime building, you can sit down with an attorney who has practiced Texas family law since 2008 and will listen to your story first.
credibilityPoints: Board Certified in Texas Family Law by the Texas Board of Legal Specialization
Practicing family law in Texas since 2008
Listed with the Texas Academy of Family Law Specialists
Community ties include CASA of Denton County and the Denton County Bar Association Adoption Day

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Navarrette Bowen, P.C. practices family law exclusively, handling divorce, child custody and visitation, modifications, marital property division, and mediation. The firm also assists with prenuptial and postnuptial agreements, adoption, protective orders, paternity, and grandparents' rights, with particular experience in high-conflict, gray, and business-owner divorce.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm is located at 525 S. Locust Street, Ste. 100 in Denton and serves clients across Denton, Collin, Wise, Tarrant, and Cooke counties. Its community ties include CASA of Denton County and the Denton County Bar Association Adoption Day.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your first step is a consultation where you tell your story and the firm asks about your goals, your family, and the facts that matter most. Consultations are paid, and you will leave with an honest picture of your options under Texas family law.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Navarrette Bowen, P.C.,

Your site already tells prospective clients that Chrysandra S. Bowen is Board Certified in Texas Family Law and has practiced since 2008, and it backs that up with named client testimonials and prominent phone and contact-form calls to action. That is a strong foundation. What no website can do on its own is answer a divorce or custody inquiry at 9 p.m., respond to the specific facts in that message, offer real consultation times, and hold the appointment with a deposit while your office is closed.

That one thing is what the IntakeBrief Lead Capturer does. We have already drafted a version for your firm, built around a custom identity sentence and a custom contact form created specifically for your practice:

"Navarrette Bowen PC is a boutique Denton family law firm where attorney Chrysandra Bowen, Board Certified in Texas Family Law and practicing since 2008, helps clients with divorce, child custody, and adoption throughout Denton County."

Here is how it works for your practice:

1. A prospective client submits the custom form. The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox, with the likely matter type identified and the sender's email set as Reply-To, so your staff can respond in one click.
2. The prospective client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message. A custody inquiry gets a custody-specific reply, not a generic acknowledgment. For a practice that handles high-conflict divorce, protective orders, and gray divorce, that first reply sets the tone your testimonials already promise.
3. The response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment. The consultation locks only after a verified payment event, which filters out no-shows before they cost your office an hour.

Your current site has no online scheduling, so every one of these steps today depends on someone being at the phone or watching the inbox. The Lead Capturer fills exactly that gap and nothing else. It does not replace your site, your staff, or your judgment about which clients to take.

We would rather you judge it by proof than by promises. We will run the complete experience for your firm for 15 days, and we invite you to test every step yourself, with your own email addresses, before you decide anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and likely matter type.
6. Confirm Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without staff action.
9. Confirm the customer receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
